game_name: Ayrton Senna's Super Monaco GP II

design_philosophy.core_loop: Qualify for races, execute lap-perfect driving across recreated F1 tracks, manage transmission settings for speed/control tradeoff, accumulate championship points across 16-race season
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Player skill execution vs. AI opponent performance; arcade-style accessibility vs. simulation depth via transmission choice
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as a Formula One driver across the 1991 F1 World Championship calendar with endorsement from then-champion Ayrton Senna; earn the 'Ayrton Senna Super License' through skill demonstration
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Directly shaped by Ayrton Senna's personal input on track design and driving physics (removed corner speed penalties from original); positions itself as authentic F1 experience through licensed tracks and driver voice commentary; arcade racing that respects sim-racing preferences through manual transmission depth

difficulty.continues_system: Qualification requirement before each race; failure to qualify results in 12th place grid penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-race structure within 16-race season; six save slots allow mid-season suspension
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Explicit Beginner/Master difficulty modes affecting both AI competitiveness and lap count (3 vs 6 laps); transmission choice (automatic/4-speed/7-speed manual) directly affects handling and top speed

progression.upgrade_system: Transmission selection per-race affecting speed/control; no persistent car upgrades documented
progression.unlock_mechanism: World Championship mode progresses through 16-race season with points accumulation; Senna GP mode unlocks upon championship completion (earning Ayrton Senna Super License after three fantasy track races)
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty/lap configurations (Beginner 3-lap, Master 6-lap, Senna GP fantasy tracks)
Free Practice mode for track learning
Six save slots enable multiple simultaneous season attempts

world_building_techniques: Ayrton Senna voice commentary on each licensed F1 track (except Circuit de Catalunya, recorded post-opening)
Licensed 1991 F1 World Championship track recreations with geographic/circuit authenticity
Senna GP fictional track trilogy as aspirational license-earning meta-narrative

environmental_storytelling: Track design reflects 1991 F1 calendar geography and layout characteristics
Rear-view HUD element creates driver immersion (simulating F1 cockpit awareness)
Tachometer/speedometer visual feedback reinforces transmission mechanical feedback

sources_used: Wikipedia - Ayrton Senna's Super Monaco GP II
